title: crepis candeli
intro: Crepis candolleana, also known as the Candolle's hawk's-beard or crepis candolleana, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Crepis candolleana include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas, decoctions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
